title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Impressionism, which emerged in France in the 1860s and 1870s, was one of the most transformative movements in the history of Western painting, (0) which marked a decisive break from the conventions of academic art. Its influence on subsequent movements in visual art has been profound and wide-reaching. (9) Although the Impressionists initially faced considerable hostility from the established critical and institutional world of French art, their work gradually gained both critical recognition and popular appeal. (10) Such was the novelty of their approach to capturing light and atmosphere that contemporaries struggled to categorise what they were seeing. These painters worked rapidly in direct observation of their subjects, and (11) Provided that they could work outdoors or in conditions of natural light, they produced images of extraordinary atmospheric freshness. Not (12) only did the Impressionists abandon the careful finish associated with academic painting, but they also radically revised the hierarchy of acceptable subject matter. (13) Regardless of the diversity of individual styles within the group, the Impressionists shared a commitment to painting the modern world in all its transience. Not (14) until the major retrospective exhibitions of the 1880s and 1890s did public opinion decisively turn in favour of what had once been widely dismissed as unfinished work. (15) Unlike the classical tradition they rejected, the Impressionists elevated the everyday scene of contemporary Parisian life to a subject worthy of serious artistic attention. This democratisation of subject matter continues to define much of the art that followed. (16) Whether Impressionism is understood primarily as a stylistic innovation or as a social document of its era, its place in the history of art is secure.

example.text: Impressionism, which emerged in France in the 1860s and 1870s, was one of the most transformative movements in the history of Western painting, (0) which marked a decisive break from the conventions of academic art.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Preposition used to introduce a contrast by highlighting the difference between two entities.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
